mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month contraction in industrial production indicates weakening aggregate supply capacity and falling corporate output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, the lagged effect of tight monetary policy suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts signals a broad-based collapse in domestic demand that amplifies recession risk beyond either variable alone, producing sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change negative and HOUST 3-month change negative
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not significant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, rising unemployment acts as a confirming signal that labor market slack is emerging, triggering earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning that drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, UNRATE increases of >0.2pp are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is not negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation erodes real corporate earnings and prompts tighter Fed policy, reducing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment growth signals robust household income and consumption trajectories, supporting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS 3-month gains > 200k are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean forward return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between high inflation and high unemployment compresses profit margins and raises the equity risk premium, producing negative returns.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of CPIAUCSL YoY > 4% and UNRATE > 5% does not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is not negative and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production from a low base signals manufacturing sector stabilization and inventory restocking, lifting cyclical earnings and generating positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0 after 3 consecutive negative months
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds after 3-month declines are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).
